title: ericameria zionis
intro: Ericameria zionis, also known as Zion's buckwheat or Zion's eriogonum, is a shrub-like plant native to the southwestern United States, particularly in the Zion National Park region. The plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ericameria zionis include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential allergic reactions and interactions with medications.
